( matnr = '150000000000009555' meinh = 'EA' ean11 = '4444-5555-6666-776' hpean = ''  )
( matnr = '150000000000009555' meinh = 'EA' ean11 = '4444-5555-6666-777' hpean = ''  )
( matnr = '150000000000009555' meinh = 'EA' ean11 = '4444-5555-6666-778' hpean = ''  )
( matnr = '150000000000009555' meinh = 'EA' ean11 = '4444-5555-6666-779' hpean = ''  )
( matnr = '150000000000009888' meinh = 'EA' ean11 = '4444-2222-6666-776' hpean = ''  )
( matnr = '150000000000009888' meinh = 'EA' ean11 = '4444-2222-6666-777' hpean = ''  )
( matnr = '150000000000009888' meinh = 'EA' ean11 = '4444-2222-6666-778' hpean = ''  )
( matnr = '150000000000009888' meinh = 'EA' ean11 = '4444-2222-6666-779' hpean = ''  )
 )

 

 

CDS View - ZMC_SELS_MEAN_NOCNT_MIN

Gets the MIN() value for each barcode.

 

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZMCSELSNOCNTMIN'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.CompareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Barcode selection count minimum'
define view ZMC_SELS_MEAN_NOCNT_MIN as select from zmc_mean_nc
{
    matnr,
    meinh,
    min(ean11) as ean11 
} group by matnr, meinh

 

 

 

Data Preview

 

 

 

VALUE #(
( matnr = '150000000000007711' meinh = 'PAL' ean11 = '4444-1111-6666-771'  )
( matnr = '150000000000009555' meinh = 'EA' ean11 = '4444-5555-6666-776'  )
( matnr = '150000000000009888' meinh = 'EA' ean11 = '4444-2222-6666-776'  )
 )

 

 

 

As you can see for the “PAL” barcode it did not bring back the main barcode but the minimum one, which is why we have the logic in the main CDS View below. In this instance that is correct, but in our main CDS view we will retrieve the main barcode if it is set.

CDS View - ZMC_SELECT_BARCODES_NC

This CDS View fetches the data per the requirement:

“Give the me the main barcode and if there isn’t one, fetch the first one.”

 

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZMCSELSBCNOCNT'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.CompareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Barcode selection no counter'
define view ZMC_SELECT_BARCODES_NC as select from ZMC_SELS_MEAN_HDR as Header

    left outer join ZMC_SELS_MEAN_NOCNT as MainBarcode
      on Header.matnr = MainBarcode.matnr
      and Header.meinh = MainBarcode.meinh
      and MainBarcode.hpean = 'X'

    left outer join ZMC_SELS_MEAN_NOCNT_MIN as BarcodeMinimum
      on Header.matnr = BarcodeMinimum.matnr
      and Header.meinh = BarcodeMinimum.meinh

{
    Header.matnr,
    Header.meinh,
    
    case when MainBarcode.ean11    is not null then MainBarcode.ean11
         when BarcodeMinimum.ean11 is not null then BarcodeMinimum.ean11
         else ''
         end as ean11
}

 

 

 

Data preview:

 

 

 

VALUE #(
( matnr = '150000000000007711' meinh = 'PAL' ean11 = '4444-1111-6666-772'  )
( matnr = '150000000000009555' meinh = 'EA' ean11 = '4444-5555-6666-776'  )
( matnr = '150000000000009888' meinh = 'EA' ean11 = '4444-2222-6666-776'  )
 )

 

 

 

Here you can see for the “PAL” material it returned the second record which is tagged as the main barcode, while for the next two materials it brought back the record with the lowest barcode value.

Conclusion

While CDS Views have no “select single” implementation, using functions like “MIN()” or “SUM()” and a bit of trickery it is possible to implement this requirement yourself.

Performance will vary with multiple factors influencing the outcome. It is up to the developer to ensure that testing covers enough scenarios to validate that the performance will be acceptable to the user.

The Report

I created an ABAP report to select from the above CDS views but also to populate the tables and you can see as an attachment to this post.

Please feel free to use it in your own learning if you like. This is not for production usage and is provided for educational purposes only.

Report output:

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Authentication と SAP S/4HANA の間でSAML2.0接続を行う
entry.content:
はじめに

本ブログでは、SAP Cloud Identity ServicesのIdentity Authentication（SAP IAS）とSAP S/4HANAの間でSAML 2.0プロトコルを使用してシングルサインオン（SSO）を構成する方法について説明します。この設定により、ユーザーは一度の認証で複数のアプリケーションにアクセスできるようになります。

目次

  1. SAML 2.0 とは？
  2. SAP IAS <-> SAP S/4HANA の接続 セットアップ手順
  3. 動作確認
  4. まとめ

 

1. SAML 2.0 とは？

SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language) 2.0 は、異なるドメイン間での認証と認可情報の交換を標準化するためのXMLベースのフレームワークです。

 

SAML 2.0の基本概念

  • アイデンティティプロバイダー (IdP)：
    • ユーザーの認証を行い、SAMLアサーションを生成するエンティティ。今回の例では、SAP IASがIdPとして機能します。
  • サービスプロバイダー (SP)：
    • IdPからのSAMLアサーションを受け取り、ユーザーにサービスを提供するエンティティ。今回の例では、SAP S/4HANAがSPとして機能します。
  • SAMLアサーション：
    • IdPが生成し、SPに送信する認証情報を含むXML文書。これにより、ユーザーが認証されたことを証明します。
  • シングルサインオン (SSO)：
    • 一度の認証で複数のサービスにアクセスできる仕組み。ユーザーは一度IdPで認証されると、他のSPにもシームレスにアクセス可能です。

 

SAML 2.0の利点

  • セキュリティの向上：
    • ユーザーのパスワードを各サービスプロバイダーに保存する必要がなく、セキュリティリスクを軽減します。
  • ユーザーエクスペリエンスの向上：
    • 一度のログインで複数のサービスにアクセスできるため、ユーザーの利便性が向上します。
  • 統合管理：
    • IdPを中心とした認証管理により、ユーザーアカウントの管理が容易になります。

 

2. SAP IAS <-> SAP S/4HANA の接続 セットアップ手順

本セクションでは、SAP IASとSAP S/4HANA の間でのSAML2.0セットアップ手順について、画面を交えてご紹介します。基本的な内容は下記SAP Help Portalに依存しますので、公式な内容はこちらをご参照ください。

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity/system-integration-guide/identity-authentication-configuration-for-sap-s-4hana

 

SAP IASのSAMLメタデータを取得する

この章では、SAP IASのSAMLデータを取得し、またSAP IASとSAP S/4HANA の間で暗号化通信を行うために、SAP IAS上で生成された証明書を取得し、そこから公開鍵を抽出するまでの手順を示します。

SAP IASのテナントにアクセスし、「Applications & Resources」->「Tenant Settings」->「SAML 2.0 Configuration」へ移動します。ここでは、SAP IASのSAML2.0に関する設定を行うことができます。

 

  1. 画面右側の「Download Metadata File」をクリックし、メタデータをダウンロードします。本ブログ内では、こちらのメタデータを「metadata_IAS.xml」として扱います。
  2. Signing Certificate セクションに証明書がない場合には「add」ボタンから追加してください。この証明書のActionからメガネマークのDisplayをクリックすると、証明書の中身を確認することができます。

  1. 証明書情報をコピーします。

  1. お手元のテキストエディタで「cert.cer」というファイルを生成し、下記のようにペーストします。

 

 

-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
<コピーした証明書の内容>
-----END CERTIFICATE-----

 

 

  1. ローカルファイルシステムで前述の「cert.cer」を保存したディレクトリに移動し、下記のコマンドを実行します。今回はopensslというツールを用いて、証明書から公開鍵を抽出し、「public_key.cer」というファイルに格納しています。

 

 

openssl x509 -in cert.cer -pubkey -noout > public_key.cer

 

 

  1. これにより、下記のような公開鍵が生成されます。なお下記に示す公開鍵は形式を表すためのものであり、ダミーです。

 

 

-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY----- MIIBIjANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQEFAAOCAQ8AMIIBCgKCAQEArVRlbWhP4Zc6XT9z+p7E 4F8o2X/o/LC/QQ08QR+F/LkE1EFi/V2YdMsa6yYB3gwn/A8Tv5e3AGsrfFL53G+o
B8MULygouF3WQ <公開鍵のイメージ> +B8MULygouF3WQB8MULygouF3WQB8MU odPUI4ML8pX5Sz1OG+NwzSHB6NxFg80URpj/D1fqu3WvVZcUGBNryICMeWXGfLhZ R7u4odCZzg+0LKwO14O6UkD4ftIJeUCNL8Ne3bX0y8WsVsAi+iwOuhF0Z0HD2V5T MRAkfw9BkTSJX4A9cwJfH/BUPsCU5kPm/xkTY2U76Y4YIozBPMDNEQa9MP7QWx/N
-----END PUBLIC KEY-----

 

 

SAP IAS上でSAP S/4HANAを指すApplicationを作成する

SAP IAS に登録するApplicationとは、SAP IAS と連携する先のアプリケーションを意味します。今回はSAP S/4HANA を追加していきます。SAP IASのテナントにアクセスし、「Applications & Resources」->「Applications」 へ移動します。

  1. Createボタンをクリックし、新しいApplicationを追加します。Applicationのタイプとして「SAP on-premise solution」 を選択します。

ABAP PlatformをSAMLサービスプロバイダーとして有効化する

この章では、SAP S/4HANAをSAMLにおけるSPとして登録するための設定を行います。

  1. SAP GUIを起動し、適切な権限を持ったユーザーとしてSAP S/4HANA にログオンしたのち、トランザクションコード 「SAML2」 を起動します。

  1. ブラウザが立ち上がります。「Enable SAML2.0 Support」 -> 「Create SAML 2.0 Local Provider」の順にクリックします。

  1. 今回、SPはSAP S/4HANA自身が該当します。したがって名前は 「S4PCE」 としました。必要に応じて、お好きな名前を入力します。「Next」をクリックします。

  1. デフォルトの設定で「Next」をクリックします。

  1. こちらもデフォルトのまま「Finish」をクリックします。

  1. これでSAP S/4HANA 自身がSPとして設定されました。したがって、次のステップとしてこのSPのメタデータをダウンロードしましょう。「metadata」をクリックします。

  1. デフォルトのまま「Download Metadata」をクリックします。今回はこのメタデータを「metadata_S4.xml」として保存します。

SAP IASをアイデンティティプロバイダーとして信頼する

  1. SAML2.0の仕組みにおいては、メタデータの相互交換が必要となります。まずはSAP S/4HANA に対して、SAP IASのメタデータをアプロードします。「Trusted Providers」 -> 「Add」 -> 「Upload Metadata File」 の順にクリックします。

  1. Metadata File をクリックして、「metadata_IAS.xml」をアップロードします。「Next」をクリックして次に進みます。

  1. アップロードされたメタデータの確認が走ります。Upload From File をクリックし、先述の「cert.cer」をアップロードします。「Next」をクリックします。

  1. デフォルトの状態で「Next」をクリックします。

  1. こちらもデフォルトで「Next」をクリックします。

  1. メタデータから抽出された、SSOに用いるエンドポイントの設定が表示されます。デフォルトで「Next」をクリックします。

  1. 同様にログアウト用のエンドポイントが表示されます。デフォルト状態で「Next」をクリックします。

  1. デフォルト状態で「Next」をクリックします。

  1. デフォルトの状態で 「Finish」 をクリックします。

ABAP Platform クライアントのName IDフォーマットを設定する

ユーザーに関する情報を共有するためには、通信先システムが異なるIDを使用している場合でも、ユーザーを識別できる必要があります。SAML 2.0規格では、共通の識別子を確立する手段として、名前識別子（Name ID）を定義しています。この章では、このName IDを設定します。

 

  1. 「Identity Federation」 -> 「Add」の順にクリックします。

  1. 今回はSAP IAS に登録されたユーザーとSPA S/4HANAに登録されたユーザーをemailアドレスによって識別したいので、「E-mail」をクリックし、「OK」により確定します。

  1. SAMLアサーション内のName IDがABAP Platform (SAP S/4HANA) 内のユーザーにどのようにマッピングされるかを設定する必要があります。このマッピングを通じて、ABAP Platformは誰がログオンしているかを識別します。SAMLアサーションで提供される情報は、ABAPプラットフォームの単一ユーザーと1対1で一致する必要があります。これを行うため、 「User ID Mapping Mode」にて、適切な項目を設定します。

  1. 最後に、「Enable」 をクリックしてIdP（SAP IAS）との間のSAML連携を有効化します。

  1. List of Trusted Providers 内の 「Active」 の部分が緑色に変化すれば完了です。

ABAP Platformクライアントをサービスプロバイダーとして信頼する

今度は逆の信頼設定を行います。

  1. SAP IASに移動し、「Applications & Resources」 -> 「<先ほど作成したS/4HANAを指すApplication>」 -> 「SAML 2.0 Configuration」 の順にクリックします。Metadata Fileの部分に先ほどダウンロードしておいた「metadata_S4.xml」をアップロードします。これにより、SAMLの設定に必要な情報が自動で読み込まれます。「Save」をクリックします。

ABAP Platformが使用するサブジェクトネーム識別子の設定

  1. 「Applications & Resources」 -> 「<先ほど作成したS/4HANAを指すApplication>」 -> 「Subject Name Identifier」 の順にクリックします。今回は「Email」を指定します。

動作確認

  1. Fiori Launchpad にアクセスすると、SAMLの設定が有効化されているため、NetWeaverの画面でIdPを選択する画面が表示されます。IdP（SAP IAS）のURLを選択し、「続行」をクリックします。

  1. SAP IAS の認証画面にリダイレクトされます。ここでは、SAP IAS に登録されたユーザーにより認証を行うことになります。

  1. SAP IAS側のユーザーとSAP S/4HANA 側のユーザーが持つ 「email」 属性によりユーザーマッピング、識別が行われ、Subject Name Identifierで指定した項目にて、SPA S/4HANA へのログオンが行われます。

まとめ

本ブログでは、SAP Cloud Identity ServicesのIdentity AuthenticationとSAP S/4HANA間でSAML 2.0を使用したSSOの設定手順について説明しました。これにより、ユーザーは一度の認証で複数のシステム間でシームレスに認証できるようになります。適切な設定とテストを行うことで、エンタープライズ環境におけるセキュリティとユーザビリティの向上が期待できます。

参考資料

  • SAP Help Portal: Identity Authentication Configuration for SAP S/4HANA
    https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity/system-integration-guide/identity-authentication-configuration-for-sap-s-4hana

entry.title: Expert Talk (Summary): Service Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
In a recent Expert Talk, SAP’s service experts and product managers, Yang Li and @GertTackaert , shared exciting updates about service capabilities within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and their product road map. Here’s a quick overview of their insights. You can watch the full video here.

Dimensions of Service Processes

We've spoken with many customers from different geographies and industries, and understand that service processes can vary greatly. We find it useful to see them in terms of the following dimensions:

  1. Where (on-site, field service vs. in-house, depot service),
  2. How (ad-hoc vs. recurring)
  3. Who (own technicians vs. subcontractors).

Key Themes from Customer

Despite the differences, service leaders consistently focus on three main themes:

  1. Operational Performance and Efficiency: Delivering more with existing resources while keeping customers happy.
  2. Automation and Transparency: Automating tasks to handle complexity and increase volume, ensuring clear visibility of the entire service process.
  3. Business Insight and Compliance: Adapting to changing business needs and ensuring both operational and financial compliance, especially in a global context.

To help service leaders tackle these challenges, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition offers capabilities that cover all service dimensions for different combinations, tailored to customers' needs.

Service as a Revenue Enabler

In the past 10-15 years, the role of services has shifted from cost centers to revenue enablers. Some companies now even drive their revenues almost exclusively through service models like subscriptions or pay-per-use. Efficient service processes are now critical to facing today’s business challenges. Our new 2023 release from October last year is a milestone for several reasons:

  • Consistent User Experience: An intuitive, end-to-end experience.
  • Real-Time Financial Reporting: Features like service margin analysis and revenue recognition.
  • New Business Models: Foundations for models like XaaS (“everything-as-a-service”) and subscription-based services.

Service Scenario Highlights

SAP S/4HANA supports various combinations of "Who," "Where," and "How" in service processes. Here are two key scenarios.

1. Field Service:

  • Steps: Customer request; quote creation; scheduling and dispatching technicians; confirmation of time, parts, and expenses; review; billing; and financial reporting.

  • Innovations: Intercompany Service Execution and Service with Advanced Execution, offering flexibility in both commercial and operational aspects of service delivery.

2. In-House Service:

  • Steps: Involves logistics for incoming/outgoing items; tracking internal movements during repairs; and billing.

  • Innovations: Enhanced process visibility and billable diagnosis, allowing companies to charge for inspection work to provide more accurate report.

You can also see a labs preview of a planned feature, which will combine in-house and field service processes into a single service flow.

 

Future Road Map

Looking ahead, our road map for Service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition includes:

  • Commercial Excellence and Financial Insight: Flexible service sales and consistent financial reporting.
  • Operational Performance: Efficient management of logistics and complex tasks.
  • Circular Economy Support: Enabling services like remanufacturing and refurbishment.

Future updates is also planned to infuse business AI to further optimize service execution processes.

 

To Learn More

To learn more about service capabilities and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in general, be sure to visit topic pages: SAP S/4HANA Service and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

entry.title: SAP ECTR for SAP S/4HANA 1.2.1 - What’s new in the new Release
entry.content:
This blog post presents four major highlights of the new SAP ECTR for SAP S/4HANA 1.2.1 release.

The following topics are essential for future-proof PLM solutions: ERP-related PLM functions and processes, the integration of capabilities from external (cloud) applications for overall cross-system processes and the topic of improving the user experience. We have already started this topics with SAP ECTR for SAP S/4HANA 1.2 and have extended it with the new release.

Now let’s look at the details of the highlights of this release 😉

 

#1 Integration of SAP PLM Requirements (BTP) in SAP ECTR

More and more customers are using systems engineering approaches to capture their product requirements in a structured way. SAP PLM at BTP enables Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) with the “Engineering” capability and supports the complete V-model. This also includes a solution that enables requirements management. The requirement models and requirements created on SAP PLM on BTP can now be seamlessly integrated into SAP ECTR and therefore boost collaboration between requirements engineers and designers. This is a real hybrid solution in which the processes and data are processed across system boundaries.

Search for Requirement Models and Requirements

In SAP ECTR it is now possible to search for requirement models and requirements. The search results can then be displayed and verified in SAP ECTR. Requirement models and requirements can be assigned to folders as well.

Link Document, Material, and Bill-of-Materials (BOM)

As an additional feature, the requirement models and requirements can be linked with Document, Material, and Bill-of-Materials (BOM) direct in SAP ECTR via drag&drop. This brings the designer's work together with the requirements.

#2 Additional Material Data

In addition to many enhancements and new features relating to the bill-of-materials (BOM) and the Change Record, we have also addressed the material master as part of the expansion of ERP-near PLM. The material master, or material for short, is the most important and powerful object in an ERP system.

The material is processed by many different departments in a company throughout the product life cycle and is enriched with a wide range of additional data. This data is an important source of information for ERP-related PLM processes and functions, but usually remains hidden from the PLM perspective. With the ERP-near extension of SAP ECTR, we uncover these valuable sources and make them available to users in SAP ECTR.

In the new release, you will find additional data for a material in a new container under the material. The additional information has been implemented as separate objects and carries the specific material data. Thanks to this technology, we are able to display all additional material data at once without having to enter organization levels (such as Plant, Storage Location, or Sales Organization).

We have currently implemented four new objects for the additional material data, which represent the following dimensions and associated information:

  • Plant Data (MARC)
  • Storage Data (MARD)
  • Accounting Data (MBEW)
  • Sales Data (MVKE)

As an example, the following screenshot shows the details of the Storage Data (MARD).

#3 New ways to copy classification

The classification of objects is essential to increase efficiency because it is often used to automate processes, increase reuse, and quite profanely, search for objects. In addition, the information from the classification represents important metadata to describe and declare a product across all facets.

Last year, with SAP ECTR for SAP S/4HANA 1.2. we have created a way to significantly simplify the classification of documents. With the new release, we did the same for materials. With this way, we help users save time and avoid errors.

Now, users can easily transfer the classification of a source object (material) to several target objects (materials), i.e. to copy the characteristic value assignment.

#4 External Window Handling

As always, improving the user experience is an important concern.

In the new release, we have implemented a significant optimization for the handling of windows in SAP ECTR, which was initiated by improvement requests from our SAP Customer Influence Campaign.

Now, each window in SAP ECTR can be used as an internal or external window. The windows status can be toggled using the new "Switch window mode" function or simple press F12. This optimizes general handling, such as working on several screens, as individual SAP ECTR windows (such as the 3D Viewer Window or the Assembly Window) can be used completely and in large format on a separate screen.

Summary

The management of product data with SAP ECTR and PLM in SAP has already become a standard in many companies that they no longer want to do without. We have pursued two main goals with the new release: Firstly, the new functionalities should further strengthen the processes in engineering and enhance functionalities and user experience. Secondly - and this is the most remarkable aspect - the extension of ERP-near PLM and the integration of SAP PLM (BTP) capabilities from the cloud. This helps companies to strategically focus on end-to-end processes and pursue an end-to-end digitalization approach.

Additional Information

Check out the SAP ECTR Road Map for further planned functions and capabilities:
Authoring Tool Integration | SAP Road Map Explorer

This blog post provides just four of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Engineering Control Center. You can find the complete list of improvements and enhancements of the release here: What's New in SAP ECTR 1.2 for SAP S4HANA | SAP Help Portal

Please share your feedback and thoughts in a comment.
You can also follow me on the SAP Community to stay up to date!
If you are interested in more PLM relevant topics, I encourage you to read other blog posts here: https://blogs.sap.com/tags/237172457501282589953116013994631/

entry.title: Standard network determination according to organization parameters
entry.content:
Requirement

We need to determine a standard project template according to organisational parameters like plant and sales organisation in assembly processing.

Solution mapping

We can cover this requirement in standard if we are going according to company code mapping for each requirement class, but if we have many plants and company codes, then it is not feasible to configure all according to requirement class and network type.

Here I am showing a way around for this requirement using a small implicit enhancement in Functional module CO_TA_TCN61_READ for Table TCN61.

After this enhancement you are able to create operative project according to plant and sales organization that I maintained in custom table that is basically a copy of standard table with extensions of two additional fields.

Custom Table

First, we need to copy table TCN61 into a custom table ZTCN61 so we can add our new fields in this table.

Go SE11 Transaction code and give table name and click on copy button.

Add fields below fields as Key fields in custom table.

 

Field

Key Field

Initial Value

Data element

Description

VKORG

X

X

VKORG

Sales Organization

WERKS

X

X

WERKS_D

Plant

 

 

Now Activate this table.

Maintenance View Setup

Here we need to maintained entry in this table for standard network selection so we need to create maintenance view and Transaction code for the same table.

Go to SE11.

Now again Click on save.

Transaction code Custom Table

Go to SE93

 Now Click on Save.

Material allocation to standard by Custom Table (ZTCN61)

Go to Transaction code ZCN08

 

Enhancement in Function Module CO_TA_TCN61_READ.

We are ready with custom table and new fields required for selection of standard network. Now we can enable enhancement code for selection of data from custom table instead of TCN61 table.

We have setup below Piece of code but as per coding standard it can be modified as required.

 

 

DATA: ls_ztcn61 TYPE ztcn61.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <ls_vbak> TYPE any.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <ls_vbap> TYPE any.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lt_xvbap> TYPE ANY TABLE.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lv_vkorg> TYPE vkorg.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lv_werks> TYPE werks_d.

 

ASSIGN ('(SAPMV45A)VBAK') TO <ls_vbak>.

ASSIGN ('(SAPMV45A)VBAP') TO <ls_vbap>.

 

IF <ls_vbak> IS ASSIGNED.

ASSIGN COMPONENT 'VKORG' OF STRUCTURE <ls_vbak> TO <lv_vkorg>.

ENDIF.

IF <ls_vbap> IS ASSIGNED.

ASSIGN COMPONENT 'WERKS' OF STRUCTURE <ls_vbap> TO <lv_werks>.

ENDIF.

 

IF <lv_vkorg> IS ASSIGNED AND <lv_werks> IS ASSIGNED.

  SELECT SINGLE *

  FROM ztcn61

  INTO ls_ztcn61

  WHERE matnr = matnr_imp

    AND auart = auart_imp

    AND vkorg = <lv_vkorg>

    AND werks = <lv_werks>.

  IF sy-subrc = 0.

    MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_ztcn61 TO tcn61_exp.

    EXIT.

  ENDIF.

ENDIF.

 

Testing of Process

 

Now we have completed the development and we need to test the process. For Testing we need to setup two different Project Templates in different plants.

Here I have setup different templates for individual Plant and sales organization but if you have multiple plants in same sales org that also we can used.

Projects Templates

For Plant 1010.

For Plant 1710

 

Note: - These templates are just for example for real scenarios I can different setup activities and WBS elements.

Material Master

Here we have setup same material master for both plants (1010 and 1710).

Sales Order and Operative Project Creation

Now we can create sales order for both plants and visualize the Operative project according respective plants that we used in sales order.

Sales Order for 1010

For Plant 1010 and Sales organization 1010.

Go Transaction code VA01 and create sales order.

 

 

Operative Project for 1010

Now Open the project created from sales order in CJ20N.

Go to Transaction code CJ20N.

Sales Order for 1710

For Plant 1710 and Sales organization 1710.

Go Transaction code VA01 and create sales order.

 

Operative Project for 1710

Now Open the project created from sales order in CJ20N.

Go to Transaction code CJ20N.

Conclusion

This enhancement is applicable to any situation in which the standard network should be derived based on parameters other than material master and network type.

 

Reference for the development

We would like to give big applause to the below blog writer from which I got the idea for this development.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/sap-ps-assembly-processing-standard-network-selection-based-on-parameters/ba-p/13279422

entry.title: Digital Transformation. View of an Enterprise Architect - Tasks of an EA
entry.content:
#GenerativeArt

In the German-speaking world, discussions about enterprise architecture and enterprise architecture management often lead to confusion. In English, a distinction is made between 'enterprise architecture' as a structure and 'enterprise architecture management' as an activity. Both concepts are often translated into German in the literature with the term "Unternehmensarchitektur" which leads to a confusion between the activity and the structure (Keller, 2017, p. 23). Enterprise architecture as a term describes the representation of the IT architecture from the perspective of the people involved, whereas enterprise architecture management describes the processes, methods, tools, responsibilities and standards that are necessary for the enterprise architecture to fulfill its intended function.

If you try to capture the tasks of enterprise architecture management by defining the activity of the role, it quickly becomes clear that many different views come together here too. In contrast to other professions, there are no protected job titles for enterprise architects. In practice, this leads to creative and inconsistent role definitions and thus to ambiguities in the role profile and tasks. Various studies deal with the analysis and evaluation of job offers. In an evaluation of the Swiss job market, Thönssen and Dewitz found 156 job advertisements for 'Enterprise Architect'. However, when analyzing the content, they found that there was no common understanding of the tasks involved (Thönssen & Von Dewitz, 2018, p. 410). A study in the Norwegian healthcare sector came to a similar conclusion (Olsen, 2017, p. 638).

Another approach to identifying the tasks of an enterprise architect was carried out by Reselman. He analyzed various IT architect roles based on certifications. He formed four categories for this: Business-, Vendor-, Developer- and Operations-oriented architects. According to his analysis, the enterprise architect is a role within the business category and is responsible for ensuring that IT systems meet the organization's goals (Reselman, 2020a). Gellweiler also chose the path of evaluation via certifications, but came to the conclusion that the knowledge imparted in certifications is often not needed in practice: "[...] only a few jobs required knowledge of or certificates for a particular framework [...]" or that some role definitions such as TOGAF do not reflect the requirements of the market: "[...] that the widely used TOGAF framework [...] does not mirror the roles as searched on labor markets" (Gellweiler, 2020, p. 27).


In conclusion, it can be stated that the tasks of the enterprise architect cannot be clearly defined. In the literature, two areas of responsibility have emerged: on the one hand, the strategic level through the coordination of IT strategies with the specialist departments and, on the other, the tactical level, which is responsible for the planning of IT infrastructure systems and IT applications. Another level that is emerging is the combination of strategic and tactical levels with a focus on innovation and customer value. The Enterprise Architects are responsible for the strategic tasks within the company. Tactical tasks, such as the specification of hardware and software components and their interaction, are carried out by solution and software architects. In practice, the design of these roles and the associated tasks are individual to each company.

 

 

Common thread of this blog series:

  1. Introduction 
  2. Tasks of an Enterprise Architect 
  3. Success Factors for Digital Transformation Projects 
  4. Help of an Enterprise-Architecture-Framework 
  5. Steps and Tips 

 

Bibliography

Gellweiler, C. (2020). Types of IT Architects: A Content Analysis on Tasks and Skills. Journal of Theoretical and Applied Electronic Commerce Research, 15(2), 0–0. https://doi.org/10.4067/S0718-18762020000200103

Keller, W. (2017). IT-Unternehmensarchitektur: Von der Geschäftsstrategie zur optimalen IT-Unterstützung (3., überarbeitete und erweiterte Auflage). dpunkt.verlag.

Olsen, D. H. (2017). Enterprise Architecture management challenges in the Norwegian health sector. Procedia VIII Computer Science, 121, 637–645. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.procs.2017.11.084

Reselman, B. (2020a). What type of IT architect are you? https://www.redhat.com/architect/what-type-it-architect-are-you

Thönssen, B., & Von Dewitz, M. (2018). A Label is not enough – Approach for an Enterprise Architecture Role Description Framework. Procedia Computer Science, 138, 409–416. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.procs.2018.10.058

entry.title: Digital Transformation. View of a Enterprise Architect - Success Factors
entry.content:
#GenerativeArt

 

In order to analyze the extent to which an enterprise architecture framework as a tool of an enterprise architect can help to manage the digital transformation, the challenges and success criteria for such a project must first be identified.

If you search in sources such as Google Scholar for terms like "Critical Success Factors" in combination with "SAP", you will find a sufficient number of sources that have already dealt with this topic. The most frequently cited criteria are as follows:


1. Clear formulation of objectives, derived from the corporate strategy
The company's mission answers the question of what benefits the company brings to affected interest groups. The vision can be derived from this in order to define how this benefit intention can be implemented. The vision therefore includes the future business models, from which the IT strategy and necessary systems are derived.


2. Project Management
The core tasks of project management are the planning of all activities required for implementation and the management and control of the project in terms of deadlines and costs. Project management is also responsible for initiating measures in the event of problems arising.


3. Consideration of all perspectives
In SAP projects, project management is usually in the hands of IT (SAP S/4HANA 2022 study, 2022, p. 24). This often leads to a subjective view and is limited to system performance or the ability to integrate into existing system landscapes. Strategic aspects such as the development of new business models, customer centricity and greater transparency are neglected as a result. There is no alternative to including all perspectives.


4. Availability of resources
Transformation projects are often carried out in parallel with day-to-day business and therefore compete with it for employee resources. For a successful project, project roles must be planned and filled at an early stage in order to prevent difficulties in the project and disruption to day-to-day business. If external resources are used for project implementation, particular importance must be attached to sharing the knowledge and objectives of all project participants.


5. Benefits of standard software
Standard software maps business processes, but also offers enough flexibility within the standard to adapt it to your own processes. Companies therefore have the option of adapting the process to the solution or adapting the solution to the process. However, they tend to be highly individualized. This increases the implementation effort and leads to a higher susceptibility to errors (Brugger et al., 2021, p. 76).

 

6. Communication of changes
The introduction of new processes and technologies changes familiar procedures. The result is uncertainty among employees, which is just as important for specialist departments as for IT. To counteract this, timely and target group-oriented communication is important to promote acceptance and understanding of the change (Kohnke et al., 2005, p. 29; Menon, 2020, p. 88).

 

7. Silo Thinking
In order to achieve a corporate goal together, it is necessary to think and work in a holistic approach in end-to-end processes. However, the majority of companies are still trapped in functional silos with their associated command and control mindset, using structures from the 19th century. This is rooted in classic hierarchical thinking. The result is power struggles, conflicting goals and 'hidden agendas' (Brugger et al., 2021, p. 77). This can be broken up by a culture of collaboration, which is exemplified by the management level, as well as the active and early involvement of affected specialist departments.

 

8. Support of the management
Sustainable management support is crucial to the success of SAP implementations. It must fully drive forward the formulated goals and strategies and make key decisions as the project progresses (Brugger et al., 2021, p. 77). A lack of management involvement can have an impact on the commitment of key users, resulting in considerable additional work for the project management. Nevertheless, management often leaves the management of SAP projects to the IT department after the project decision has been made (Boban et al., 2016, p. 1338).

 

 

 

Common thread of this blog series:

  1. Introduction 
  2. Tasks of an Enterprise Architect 
  3. Success Factors for Digital Transformation Projects 
  4. Help of an Enterprise-Architecture-Framework 
  5. Steps and Tips 

 

 

Bibliograph

Boban, S., Stevanovic, V., Vujovic, V., & Denic, N. (2016). Key factors for successful implementation of ERP systems. Tehnicki Vjesnik - Technical Gazette, 23(5). https://doi.org/10.17559/TV-20150618213311

Brugger, T., Czeslik, M., Hager, A., & Uebel, M. (2021). Business Transformation mit S/4HANA: Leitlinien und Vorgehensmodell für einen ganzheitlichen Unternehmenswandel. Springer Fachmedien Wiesbaden. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-658-33968-5

Kohnke, O., Bungard, W., & Madukanya, V. (2005). Change Management im Rahmen von SAP-Implementie- rungsprojekten Eine empirische Studie.pdf. Gabler.

Menon, S. (2020). Critical Success Factors for ERP Projects: Recommendations from a Canadian Exploratory Study. International Journal of Business and Management, 15(2), 80. https://doi.org/10.5539/ijbm.v15n2p80

Studie SAP S/4HANA 2022. (2022). https://www.lufthansa-industry-solutions.com/de-de/studien/studie-2022- sap-s4hana

entry.title: Digital Transformation. View of an Enterprise Architect - Help
entry.content:
#GenerativeArt

 

One of the main questions in the discussion about enterprise architecture is always whether an enterprise architecture framework helps to manage the digital transformation. To answer this question, I have analyzed the extent to which an EAF supports the success factors from Part 2. I used TOGAF as the reference framework, as it is currently the best-known and most widely used framework (Hanschke, 2022, p. 32; Reselman, 2020b).

 

1. Clear formulation of objectives
In order to ensure that the enterprise architecture to be developed follows the corporate goals and strategy, "board strategies, business plans, business strategy, IT strategy, business principles, business goals, and business drivers" are queried as input parameters (Josey & Hornford, 2022, p. 113). These serve as the basis for planning the architecture development and thus ensure that the architecture work is in line with the business objectives. Although this does not support the creation of the target formulation itself, its results are requested as input for the architecture work.

 

2. Project Management
TOGAF devotes an entire chapter to the topic of project management (TOGAF® Series Guide: Architecture Project Management, 2022). However, this relates purely to the integration of existing project management methods into the TOGAF process model. TOGAF therefore does not offer its own project management methodology "[...] on how to manage an Architecture-Project and leaves that to methods that focus on Project Management" (TOGAF® Series Guide: Architecture Project Management, 2022, p. 3).

 

3. Consideration of all perspectives
By dividing the enterprise architecture into different architecture domains, TOGAF forces the architecture to be viewed from different perspectives. Strategy, processes, organization and information requirements are considered in the business architecture. In particular, the interests of those involved with regard to the target architecture are taken into account here. The data architecture supports the structural aspects including the type of data processing. The application architecture focuses on the definition of the systems and their interaction, and the technology architecture designs a view of the infrastructure components as the basis for the implementation phase. Changes that occur during the sometimes very long project runtimes are managed by "architecture change management" and thus also taken into account. Combining these perspectives enables a holistic view of the organization and ensures that the business objectives are achieved.
Critically, however, it should not be forgotten that TOGAF was designed for the architectural development of information systems and technologies. Organizational culture or employee engagement, for example, are not considered.


4. Availability of resources
To carry out architecture development, employees with specific skills in the field of enterprise architecture work are required. These are often not available in the companies and must first be hired or trained. In addition, there is sometimes intensive involvement of relevant people during the project phase. This can lead to bottlenecks in day-to-day tasks or reduced availability of these resources in the architecture project. In line with this, considerable costs are incurred during the development and implementation of an enterprise architecture, which must also be planned and available as a resource for the project. Through clearly structured planning and implementation of enterprise architecture development in ADM and the associated clear definition of roles and responsibilities, TOGAF reduces these resource costs as much as possible.

 

5. Benefits of standard software
As an independent framework, TOGAF does not consider specific standard software. However, it provides a structured framework for the planning, development and implementation of IT architectures. This can be used to support the introduction of standard software and ensure integration into the overall architecture. By defining the architecture principles, processes and tools within ADM, the advantages and disadvantages of standard software are sufficiently analyzed.


6. Communication
TOGAF is a framework that helps with the structuring and planning of IT architectures. It creates transparency about the target state of the company to be achieved and can thus eliminate misunderstandings or concerns about the direction of the company. Through holistic stakeholder management, it involves those affected at an early stage and reduces uncertainty. However, since employee uncertainty is a highly complex, organizational issue, TOGAF alone is not sufficient in the author's opinion.

 

7. Silo Thinking
One of the key points of TOGAF is the involvement of all persons concerned in the architecture process. This promotes a culture of collaboration and breaks down barriers between different teams and departments. The different, standardized views of the business, data, application and technology architecture are combined in an overall architecture. This approach ensures the integration of all areas, resulting in the breaking down of silos. With TOGAF, architectural elements are reused across different parts of the organization, thus avoiding redundant or isolated solutions.

 

8. Management support
The TOGAF standard recommends the establishment and operation of an Enterprise Architecture Board to ensure that the organization acts in accordance with the principles set out in the architecture. This board must be able to make decisions and be held accountable for them. It should therefore be made up of management representatives from various interest groups. To ensure that the enterprise architecture board is involved in all major decisions, artifacts are created during various phases of ADM that must be signed by the enterprise architecture board in the form of architecture contracts. These include, for example, the architecture contract in phase A of the ADM or the architecture principles that are defined in the preliminary phase (Josey & Hornford, 2022, p. 138). This approach ensures sustainable management support.

 

 

In summary, the question of whether TOGAF helps with the digital transformation can be answered with a clear "Jein" in German or with an "it depends". TOGAF is a very comprehensive and generic framework that supports 5 of the 8 success factors. However, it should not be forgotten that this holistic approach also leads to a high level of complexity and rigidity, which requires a lot of time and resources. Spontaneous use is therefore not possible (Hanschke, 2022, p. 36). If you come across the argument in discussions that there is tailoring for this, you must also bear in mind that individual tailoring is itself a project and can only be carried out by a resource that already has extensive experience in dealing with TOGAF.

So its use must be considered on an individual basis and makes no sense if the use of TOGAF takes up more time than the complete rest of the project.

If it is fine for you to use a Vendor-Specific Framework, I would recommend to have a look at the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework (SAP EAF). Within SAP EAF, TOGAF forms the basis for the phases, activities and artifacts but SAP EAF extends the TOGAF metamodel with SAP-specific concepts and offers additional methods and tools that have been specially developed for SAP integration. You can find a description here. 

 

 

Common thread of this blog series:

  1. Introduction 
  2. Tasks of an Enterprise Architect 
  3. Success Factors for Digital Transformation Projects 
  4. Help of an Enterprise-Architecture-Framework 
  5. Steps and Tips 

 

 

 

Bibliograph

Hanschke, I. (2022). Enterprise Architecture Management – einfach und effektiv: Ein praktischer Leitfaden für die Einführung von EAM. Carl Hanser Verlag GmbH Co KG.

Josey, A., & Hornford, D. (2022). The TOGAF® Standard, 10th Edition—A Pocket Guide. The Open Group.

Reselman, B. (2020b, September 14). TOGAF and the history of enterprise architecture. https://www.redhat.com/architect/togaf

entry.title: Fiscal Year Variant
entry.content:
Fiscal Year Variant:

Defines Financial Year, Defines how many normal periods and special periods for the financial year.

Normal Financial year contains 12 normal periods plus up to 4 Special Periods

Normal periods always start from period1 to 12

Normal periods contains the period definitions.

Special periods does not contains the period definitions

Period definition :

It defines each period start date and end date of the period

Example

Period1--->from April1 to April 30th

Period2 ----> From 1st May to 31st May

Period12-----> From 1st March to 31st March

Type of the Fiscal Year:

1.Calendar Fiscal Year

2.Non Calendar Fiscal Year

1.Calendar Fiscal Year:

Those Financial Year Posting Period should be equal to Calendar Month.

Example: Period1 = Jan , Period2 = Feb , Period3 = Mar , Period4 = Apr , Period5 = May , Period6 = June , Period7 =July Period12 = Dec

Every Period Start Date and End Date should be equal to Calendar Month that type of Financial Year can be called as Calendar Year.

Period1----> 1st jan to 31st jan

Period2----> 1st Feb to 28th feb or 29th Feb

Period3 ----< 1St March to 31st March

 

Period12----> 1st Dec to 31st Dec

Note :

Not required to define any period definition

System follows standard functionality if FY defined / activated with Calendar year indicator

In the Calendar Fiscal Year, the Posting Periods and Fiscal Year Determines based on Posting Dates

Posting date : 20.03.2022======> system determines Posting Period =3 and Fiscal Year =2022

Not required any mapping rules for period definitions in case of FY as a calendar year

2.Non Calendar Fiscal Year:

Other than Jan to Dec FY can be called as Non Calendar year

Example :  April to March

                 July to June

                 Oct to Sept

Even though FY follows Jan to dec but period definition not equal to calendar month start date and end date

FY====> 1st Jan to 31st Dec

Period 1------> 1st Jan to 29th Jan             not equal to calendar Month # Jan

Period2-------> 30th Jan to 28th Feb          not equal to calendar Month # Feb

Period3-------> 29th Feb to 30th March      not equal to calendar Month # March

Note :

Posting date : 20.03.2022====> How the system knows what is the posting period = ???? What is the Fiscal year = ???

in case of non calendar FY , Need to specify the period definitions to get the Posting period and FY

In case of Non-Calendar Year ,the Posting Period and Fiscal Year determine based on posting date along with Fiscal Year Variant Configuration Settings

Definition :

In Case of Non-Calendar Year, we need to maintain relationship between Calendar Month and Calendar Day and Posting Period 

 Calendar monthCalendar Day FY PeriodYear Shift Indicator
Jan13110-1
Feb22911-1
Mar33112-1
Apr4301 
May5312 
Jun6303 
July7314 
Aug8315 
Sept9306 
Oct10317 
Nov11308 
Dec12319 

Year Shift Indicator:

in case of Calendar FY====> Not applicable year shift indicator

in case of Non Calendar FY====> applicable / required year shift indicator

Calendar FY 

 Example 2021----> Period1 (01.01.2021 to 31.01.2021) …….. Period12 (01.12.2021 to 31.12.2021)

in the above case , FY Periods and calendar FY are identical

Calendar Year ===>2021=====> FY 2021===> Periods and calendar months are identical

In the above case , not required year shift indicator functionality

Non calendar FY

Few periods belong to One calendar year and few periods belongs to other calendar year

Example : FY 2021 Apr to March 

Period  Calender Year FYYear shift indicator

1101.02.2022 to 29.02.2022-1

Business requirement===> FY 2022=====> Oct 2021 to Sept2022

Example : Define FY Oct to Sept

 Calendar MonthCalendar DayFY periodYear Shift Indicator
Jan13140

April43070

Note :

Need to use year shift indicator to bring the different calendar year periods under one Fiscal year for non calendar FY.

Maintain Fiscal Year Variant:

SPRO -> SAP Reference IMG -> Financial Accounting -> Financial Accounting Global Settings -> Ledgers -> Fiscal Year and Posting Periods -> Maintain Fiscal Year Variant (OB29)

Maintaining standard FV for Company Code

Assign Company code to a Fiscal Variant:

SPRO -> SAP Reference IMG -> Financial Accounting -> Financial Accounting Global Settings -> Ledgers -> Fiscal Year and Posting Periods -> Assign Company Code to a Fiscal Variant (OB37)

Note: Creating our own Fiscal variants

Z1 --> FY var for Calendar Year -->  Jan to dec

Z2 --> FY Apr to March

Period Text=====> Its optional

==> Possible to define our own text both in English , Local languages

==> It follows the standard text if not maintained your own text

 

Year Independent FY Variant:

Can be defined either calendar year or Non calendar year

FY period definition is constant irrespective of the Fiscal year

Period definition it wouldn't vary depends on the FY

Example :

FY===> Apr to march

FY===> Jan to Dec

FY===> July to June

FY===> Oct to Sept

 

 Calendar monthCalendar Day FY PeriodYear Shift Indicator
Jan13110-1
Feb22911-1
Mar33112-1
Apr4301 
May5312 
Jun6303 
July7314 
Aug8315 
Sept9306 
Oct10317 
Nov11308 
Dec12319 

                                                  FY ===> Apr to March

Note : In the above case , the above period definition not specified any specific to FY===> these type of FY can be called as year Independent FY

Year dependent FY Variant:

Possible to define only Non calendar years

FY period definition is not constant and period definition it may vary depends on the Fiscal year

Period definition it may vary depends on the FY

Example1 :Define FY Jan to Dec and every period should end with last working day of the calendar month only Sunday is Holiday.

13110  ===> 31.12.2023 till 31.01.2024

43040  ===>31.03.2024 till 30.04.2024
53150  ====> 01.05.2024 till 31.05.2024
62960  ===>  01.06.2024 till 29.06.2024
73170  ===> 30.06.2024 till 31.07.2024.
83180  ===> 01.08.2024 till 31.08.2024
93090  ===> 01.09.2024 till 30.09.2024
1031100  ===> 01.10.2024 till 31.10.2024
1130110  ===> 01.11.2024 till 30.11.2024
1231120  ===> 01.12.2024 till 31.12.2024

Short End Fiscal Year:

Those fiscal year posting periods less than the 12 calendar months can be called as shortened fiscal year.

Fiscal year Conversions ( Specific year conversion)

      Business Use New case :

            Newly incorporation of a company or newly starting a business during the year.

            Winding up and merging of the company during the year, the specific year becomes the shortened              FY

    Shifting from Calendar year to Non Calendar Year or Vice versa.

            FY Jan to dec====> FY Oct to Sept

            FY Jan to dec====> FY APR to March

   Shifting from Non Calendar year to Calendar Year

          FY Oct to Sept====> FY Jan to Dec

         FY Apr to March ====> FY Jan to Dec 

Business Example :

Business need to shift from Jan to dec into APR to March

FY2020Jan 2020 to Dec 2020   ======> Full Financial YearYear dependent FY
FY2021Jan 2021 to  March 2021  ======> Shortened  FYYear dependent FY
FY2022APR 2021  to March2022   ======> Full Financial YearYear dependent FY
FY2023APR 2022  to March2023   ======> Full Financial YearYear dependent FY

Process steps to be followed

1.Need to complete all the period end activities

   Month end closing process to be completed

   FI Period end process

   CO period end process

2.Apply the FY conversions

   Use the Same FY variant code ( example Y8)

   Need to activate Year dependent FY indicator , Short FY indicator for FY variant Y8 for the year 2021

   Need to define Year dependent FY variant for year 2021

   Need to define Year dependent FY for future atleast to 10 years to 20years ( like 2022, 2023, 2024 till 2041)

   Need to define Year dependent FY for past atleast 10years to 20 years back ( like 2019,2018,2017,2016,2015 -------->1990)

3.Need to perform settings in Asset accounting

  Asset Accounting Configuration relating to Fiscal year change

   Step1: Define Reduction rules for shortened Fiscal Year

   Path: SPRO->Financial Accounting->Asset Accounting->Valuation->Fiscal Year->Shortened Fiscal Years->Define Reduction Rules for Shortened Fiscal Years

   Step2: Generate Period Control Rules

   Path: Financial Accounting->Asset Accounting ->Depreciation->Valuation Methods->Period Control->Generate Period Controls OA84

  Step3: No reduction is Short year for depreciation key

  Path: SPRO->Financial Accounting->Asset Accounting->Valuation->Depreciation->Valuation Methods->Depreciation Key->Maintain Depreciation Key AFAMA

4.Need to perform period end process again FI & CO

Difficulties :

Dump / run time errors in the settlement of orders / asset accounting

These issues resolved by applying the SAP OSS ( Support) notes

Current FY ====> Calender Year as below

Now need to shift from Jan to dec to April to march from year 2021 onwards

     FY conversions to be defined as a Year dependent FY

     Base year to be defined as Shorted FY

     FY 2021====> Jan 2021 till March 2021====> No of periods==>3Periods

Save 

FY 2020

FY2020===> Jan 2020 to Dec 2020

Save

Do not save then click on Fiscal year Variant

Maintain for year 2022

Do not save then click on Fiscal Year variant

Save
